global.title: Swiss Associations are non-commercial entities that cannot receive equity investment or distribute profits.
global.content:
Swiss Associations are a flexible, cost-efficient legal form well suited to open-source and public-goods teams. They offer:

- Independent legal personhood
- Privacy protections (members are not listed in a public registry)
- Democratic governance
- International recognition
- Low administrative overhead

Critically, a Swiss Association is non-commercial — it cannot carry out exclusively commercial activities, receive equity investments, or distribute profits. For teams receiving grants, paying contributors, and building public goods, this constraint is actually the right fit.

Contributors work through the entity, not as individuals — avoiding the risk of being treated as a general partnership where each person carries unlimited personal liability. While identities are known to legal counsel (attorney-client privilege) and the operator for KYB/KYC compliance, they are not publicly disclosed.

A legal entity protects against the majority of operational and contractual liability but does not protect against gross negligence, willful misconduct, or criminal liability.
global.description: Non-commercial form offering legal personhood, privacy (no public member registry), democratic governance, and low admin overhead.
